name: read_file
description:
Read the text content of a specific file from the user's Google Drive.

Use this tool after google_drive_search ONLY to glance at it's rough content,
for example to summarize, answer a question, decide whether the file is relevant, or
cite a specific passage. 

Content returned here is lossy: layout, images,
formulas, formatting, and anything beyond a character/line cap are
discarded, and binary file types are not readable at all.

Supports Google Docs (exported as Markdown), Google Sheets (exported as CSV),
Google Slides (exported as plain text), PDFs, and other text-based files.
Binary files (images, videos, zip) cannot be read by this tool.

For large files, use start_line/end_line to read specific sections, or rely on
max_chars to truncate. When content is truncated, inform the user and offer to
read more.

When presenting file content:
- Summarize or answer the user's question based on the content
- Use the render_inline_citation tool to cite specific parts
- If the file is too large, read the most relevant section first

file_id.description: The Google Drive file ID to read (from google_drive_search results)

max_chars.description: Maximum number of characters to return (default 100000, max 500000). Ignored when start_line/end_line are set.

start_line.description: Optional start line (1-based inclusive) for reading a specific range

end_line.description: Optional end line (1-based inclusive) for reading a specific range
